# Offsend Radar

> Weekly public research on AI-context hygiene in open-source repositories — path-level checks, anonymous fleet aggregates, no secrets collected.

Offsend Radar is part of [Offsend](https://offsend.io/), a local-first CLI for controlling what AI coding tools can read. The dashboard publishes sanitized weekly scan aggregates at https://radar.offsend.io.

## Key pages

- [Latest report](https://radar.offsend.io/): Most recent fleet scan — repos scanned, AI ignore coverage, risk signal categories
- [Archive](https://radar.offsend.io/archive/): All published weekly reports
- [Methodology](https://radar.offsend.io/methodology/): What is scanned, what is not scanned, and how results are sanitized
- [Participants](https://radar.offsend.io/participants/): Repositories whose maintainers opted in to named listings
- [GitHub](https://github.com/Offsend/radar): Orchestrator source and opt-in instructions

## Methodology summary

- Path-level checks only — no file contents read, no secrets collected
- Fleet aggregates are anonymous by default; repository names appear only with maintainer opt-in
- Scans use the same public-safe `offsend report` mode as the Offsend CLI
- Reports cover exposed path categories (e.g. .env, key material) and AI ignore file coverage (.cursorignore, .claudeignore, etc.)
- Radar does not certify security or claim repositories are secret-free

## Reproduce locally

```bash
brew install --cask offsend/tap/offsend-cli
offsend show
offsend report . --out report.json
```

Everything runs locally. No file contents are uploaded.

## Contact

- Product: https://offsend.io
- Source: https://github.com/Offsend/radar
